reasoning: The core driver of OXY's recent massive surge (from ~$54 to $65.32) is a major geopolitical event (US-Israel war on Iran leading to the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz in March 2026). Given this unprecedented supply shock, oil prices have spiked dramatically, and OXY has seen strong upward momentum. While such steep rallies are often followed by profit-taking and mean reversion, the ongoing nature of a major war and blockade makes a sudden resolution within the next 4 trading days highly unlikely. (Note that April 3, 2026, is Good Friday, so the market will close on Thursday, April 2). Without an immediate ceasefire, the risk premium on oil and related stocks will remain extremely high, and the short-term trend is more likely to continue or consolidate than to sharply reverse. The strong momentum edge slightly outweighs the mean-reversion risk, yielding a probability just above 50%.
